merge_groups: Trade policy news into Economy category
plan.processing_strategy: Clustered by domain, merged duplicates, prioritized high UPSC-yield topics

readme.summary: Climate policy changes dominate news. Budget implementation shows sectoral shifts. International trade tensions escalate.
key_trends: Increasing focus on renewable energy transition
exam_strategy_notes: Climate policy is VERY HIGH priority - appeared 4 consecutive days
Budget implementation details essential for Mains GS-III
Trade tensions require understanding of WTO and bilateral agreements
readme.data_quality_notes: High quality news sources, minimal noise

topics.title: Global Climate Summit Agreement on Emissions Targets
topics.why_in_news: World leaders reached consensus on binding emissions reduction targets by 2030
topics.core_concept: International climate agreements, NDCs, and emission trading mechanisms
topics.explanation: A landmark climate summit produced a binding agreement on emissions reduction. All participating nations committed to nationally determined contributions (NDCs) with verification mechanisms. The agreement includes financial support for developing nations and technology transfer provisions. This represents the most comprehensive climate accord since Paris, with enforcement mechanisms.

mcq.question: The recent global climate summit agreement sets binding emissions reduction targets for which year?

mains.question: Discuss the significance of the latest global climate agreement and its implications for India's renewable energy goals.

answer_framework.intro: Introduce the recent climate summit and binding agreement
body: Key provisions: emissions targets, finance mechanism, technology transfer
answer_framework.conclusion: India's role in global climate action while meeting development needs

topics.revision_note_50_words: Global climate summit established binding 2030 emission targets (45% reduction from 2020). $100B annual climate finance. Tech transfer for renewables. Verification mechanisms included. Most comprehensive agreement since Paris Accord. Critical for India's net-zero goals.
topics.static_link: Paris Agreement, NDC, Emission Trading System, UNFCCC, Net Zero Commitments

topics.title: Union Budget 2026: Sectoral Allocation and Tax Changes
topics.why_in_news: Finance Minister announced major shifts in budget allocation and tax policy
topics.core_concept: Fiscal policy, budget allocation, tax structure, fiscal deficit management
topics.explanation: The Union Budget 2026 introduces significant changes to sectoral spending and tax policy. Infrastructure receives 22% allocation, while new incentives for startups and MSMEs provide tax breaks. The fiscal deficit target remains at 4.5% of GDP. Enhanced allocation for healthcare and education reflects social spending priorities.

mcq.question: The fiscal deficit target in Union Budget 2026 is set at what percentage of GDP?

mains.question: Analyze the impact of Budget 2026's fiscal policy on inflation management and economic growth.

answer_framework.conclusion: Balanced approach to fiscal sustainability and growth

topics.revision_note_50_words: Union Budget 2026: Infrastructure 22%, healthcare 3%, education 4%. Fiscal deficit 4.5% of GDP. MSME tax incentives for 5 years. Enhanced social spending focus. Maintains fiscal prudence while promoting growth.
topics.static_link: Fiscal Policy, Budget, Fiscal Deficit, Tax System, Monetary Policy

topics.title: India-China Border Talks Resume After 6-Month Hiatus
topics.why_in_news: Military dialogue resumed at Moldo for LAC disengagement discussions
topics.core_concept: Bilateral relations, border management, BIMSTEC, regional stability
topics.explanation: Military talks between India and China resumed at Moldo after 6 months of suspension. Focus areas include LAC disengagement, military transparency, and establishing buffer zones. Both nations expressed commitment to peace and stability, though substantive progress remains limited. The dialogue signals diplomatic engagement but underlying disputes persist.

key_facts: Talks held at Moldo, eastern Ladakh

mcq.question: Where was the India-China military dialogue held after the 6-month hiatus?

mains.question: Discuss the significance of India-China military talks for regional peace and India's strategic positioning in Asia.

answer_framework.intro: Background of LAC tensions and previous agreements

topics.revision_note_50_words: India-China military talks resumed at Moldo after 6-month gap. Focus: LAC disengagement, military transparency, buffer zones. Positive signal but substantive progress limited. Strategic importance for South Asian stability.
topics.static_link: LAC, Indo-China Relations, BIMSTEC, Strategic Autonomy, Regional Balance
